title: Ensure that the --encryption-provider-config argument is set as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the --encryption-provider-config argument is set as appropriate
input_row.rationale: etcd is a highly available key-value store used by Kubernetes deployments for persistent storage of all of its REST API objects. These objects are sensitive in nature and should be encrypted at rest to avoid any disclosures. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --encryption-provider-config argument is set to a EncryptionConfig file. Additionally, ensure that the EncryptionConfig file has all the desired resources covered especially any secrets.
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and configure a EncryptionConfig file. Then, edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml on the master node and set the --encryption-provider-config parameter to the path of that file: --encryption-provider-config=</path/to/EncryptionConfig/File> Default Value: By default, --encryption-provider-config is not set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/administer-cluster/encrypt-data/ 2. https://acotten.com/post/kube17-security 3. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 4. https://github.com/kubernetes/features/issues/92
input_row.profile_applicability: •  Level 1 - Master Node •  Level 2 - Master Node

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_encryption_provider_config_set

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **Kubernetes API calls or kubectl SDK methods to use:**
  - Use `kubectl get pod -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o yaml` to retrieve the manifest of the kube-apiserver running on the control plane.
  - Alternatively, directly inspect the static pod manifest file at `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` if accessible via automation.

- **Kubernetes CLI commands (if applicable):**
  - `kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].command}'`
  - Or, for clusters using static pods (most kubeadm-based clusters): parse `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` for the `--encryption-provider-config` flag.

- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  - kube-apiserver pod/container command arguments.

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - The presence of the `--encryption-provider-config=<path>` argument in the kube-apiserver command line.
  - The referenced EncryptionConfig file should exist at the specified path (if file system access is available).
  - (Optional advanced check) Parse the EncryptionConfig file to ensure that all sensitive resources (especially `secrets`) are covered by an encryption provider.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** The kube-apiserver is started with the `--encryption-provider-config` flag set to a valid file path.
  - **Fail:** The `--encryption-provider-config` flag is missing or empty.

- **Example:**
  - Extract the kube-apiserver command line and verify that `--encryption-provider-config` is present and points to a file.
  - (Optional) If the file is accessible, parse it to ensure resources like `secrets` are listed under `resources:` and have a non-identity provider (e.g., `aescbc`, `kms`, etc.).

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.7.1 PDF
